title: Resume
content:
Experience# iManage# Senior Technical Writer June 2026 - Present
Write, test, and structure end-user documentation for the iManage platform, covering navigation, user settings, and management of clients, workspaces, and documents Varonis Systems# Technical Content Manager, Cloud Security
January 2025 – October 2025
Created and edited technical blogs, whitepapers, and content focused on cloud security, APIs, and SaaS platforms Improved content retrieval and visibility using metadata tagging and structured authoring principles Collaborated with researchers, designers, and engineers to deliver content aligned with enterprise goals Managed content workflows and performance in Asana and HubSpot Partnered on AI-assisted content projects, refining tone, accuracy, and style to improve retrieval by LLMs IBM# Information Developer, Technical Writer
Oct 2021 – Oct 2024
Authored developer-focused documentation for Kubernetes, OpenShift, and cloud security features Translated technical concepts in identity, container security, and infrastructure automation into practical guidance for architects, developers, and IT administrators Collaborated with engineering, UX, and documentation teams to ensure content meets security standards, accessibility guidelines, and product strategy alignment Designed modular and reusable documentation structures to support scalability across cloud services and internal knowledge bases Improved API documentation for clarity, structure, and developer usability Used developer feedback and content performance insights to improve clarity, usability, and technical accuracy Applied Research Associates (ARA)# Staff Technical Writer
Jul 2020 – Oct 2021
Created and edited instructional content, proposals, and presentations for technical and government audiences Streamlined templates and editorial workflows to ensure brand alignment and consistency across deliverables Collaborated with subject matter experts to transform dense content into accessible, outcome-focused materials Developed structured documentation for technical programs and research systems Supported cross-functional engineering teams with high-quality written deliverables Maintained documentation standards across multiple projects NC State University – Office of Information Technology# Technical Writing Intern
Jul 2019 – May 2020
Wrote user guides, support documentation, and web content. Helped streamline internal documentation workflows. NC State University – DELTA# Technical Writing Intern
Oct 2018 – Jul 2019
Created instructional and support content for educational technologies. Improved clarity and task-based learning materials. Purdue University Writing Lab# Undergraduate Business Writing Consultant
Aug 2016 – Dec 2017
Assisted students with business and technical writing. Provided guidance on clarity, structure, and audience-focused communication.

title: Writing Samples
content:
Writing Samples# A selection of published work. Everything here is publicly available — links go to the live docs and articles.
Note: Documentation is a living product — pieces published during my time at IBM (2021–2024) have continued to evolve since. Links reflect the current published versions.
Product & Concept Documentation# About IBM Cloud Object Storage — Product overview written for both technical and non-technical readers. Satellite storage templates overview — UX-focused conceptual content designed to improve onboarding and reduce support friction. OpenShift Data Foundation on Satellite — Task-based configuration guide for a deeply technical audience. API Documentation# IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service API — Create a Satellite cluster — REST API reference documentation: endpoint behavior, parameters, and payload structure for cluster infrastructure. IBM Cloud Object Storage — S3-compatibility API — Developer-facing guide mapping IBM COS behavior to the S3 API, written for engineers migrating existing tooling. Tutorials & Walkthroughs# How to create an S3 bucket — Step-by-step product walkthrough with screenshots and context on why each setting matters. Security & Industry Writing (Varonis)# ForcedLeak — Research writeup translating original security research for a broad audience. What is Database Activity Monitoring (DAM)? — Explainer on a data protection category. The Salesloft Drift breach and SaaS risk — News analysis connecting a breach to broader SaaS security lessons. DSPM and Kubernetes — Bridging data security posture management and container platforms.
